data.title: LendingTree announces $475M credit facility
data.description:
LendingTree (NASDAQ:TREE) announced on Friday a $475M credit facility, consisting of a $400M term loan B and a $75M revolving credit facility with five-year maturities.

Shares were +1.19% pre-market to $63.90.

The $400M loan carries an interest rate of SOFR + 450 bps and the $75M revolver an interest rate of SOFR + 350 bps.

Proceeds are expected to be used to refinance existing debt and for general corporate purposes.

The financing replaces the company's existing term loan B due 2028 and a loan agreement with Apollo.

"The new facility will reduce our interest expense, eliminate several restrictive covenants from prior agreements, and reward shareholders with a lower cost of capital that is reflective of our improved growth trajectory," said CFO Jason Bengel.

data.title: Zoom perks up as analysts identify healthy outlook, AI adoption trends
data.description:
Zoom's (NASDAQ:ZM) third quarter fiscal 2026 financial results featured the highest year-over-year revenue growth in 11 quarters, bolstered by its Enteprise segment and artificial intelligence tools.

The stock climbed 4% during early Friday trading as most financial firms came away impressed by the results and outlook.

"Enterprise rev of $730.7m (+7% y/y) was $12.3m ahead of consensus (1.7% upside)," said Jefferies analysts, led by Samad Samana, in a Friday investor note. "The firm is continuing to benefit from ARPU expansion into large enterprises, with $100k+ in TTM rev customers modestly accelerating to +9% y/y (32% of total rev). From a product standpoint, $100k+ ARR Workvivo customers grew to 168 (+142% y/y) and $100k + ARR ZCC customers grew to 229 (+94% y/y)."

Jefferies maintained its Buy rating and $100 price target.

Similarly, Wedbush reiterated its Outperform rating and $95 price target following the results.

"ZM AI Companion remains a strong spot in the business with MAUs growing 4x y/y as this feature provides new and improved AI use cases built into the platform with enterprises now expanding their AI use cases beyond basic meeting summaries with more momentum for its AI integration across multiple products including Zoom Phone and Zoom Docs," said Wedbush analysts, led by Daniel Ives, in a note. 

Meanwhile, Oppenheimer maintained its Perform rating, but did not assign a price target. They noted Zoom's transition to an "AI-first" company appears to be working.

"AI Companion's usage is extending beyond meeting summaries to include answering users' questions, scheduling and preparing meetings, searching information, and building content," said Oppenheimer analyst Timothy Horan, in a note. "ZM is monetizing AI through its Custom AI Companion offering, which is reportedly earning positive customer reception."

Wells Fargo retained its Hold rating on the stock and an $80 price target as they are unsure if Zoom can maintain its growth reacceleration.

Stifel also maintained its Hold rating but reduced its price target to $80 from $85.

"Zoom has effectively seeded the market with AI functionalities, with 'millions' of AI Companion users, and continues to ramp monetization, most meaningfully through Contact Center Elite and ZVA today," said Stifel anlaysts, led by J. Parker Lane, in a note. "Finally, while management noted that macro conditions remain dynamic, the company observed a partial abatement of the heightened scrutiny in certain geographies that was noted in 1Q."

Zoom competes against much larger companies that offer video conferecing platforms, such as Microsoft (MSFT) Teams, Cisco's (CSCO) Webex and Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) Meet. Shares for these stocks were up 0.1%, 0.5% and 1.5%, respectively.

Zoom's annual flagship event, Zoomtopia, is scheduled to run September 17-18.

data.title: Banks lobby US regulators for national standards - report
data.description:
U.S. banks are asking the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency to create national standards for banking services that would take precedence over state banking rules, according to a media report on Friday.

Large banks, specifically, are lobbying for uniform U.S. regulations on lending, bond issuance, providing investment bank services, or assessing anti-money risks, essentially limiting state power over their operations, Reuters reported, citing three people familiar with the matter.

The push for national standards comes as the banking industry is seeking more favorable national rules, such as capital requirements, while the Trump administration provides a more favorable environment for easing regulatory burdens on corporate America.

The broader standards would reign in states' ability to disrupt banks' operations with rules on so-called "de-banking," in which lenders allegedly deny services to customers for political or religious beliefs, the article said. 

Banks plan to increase their lobbying efforts after meeting with the OCC earlier this year to advance the issue, the three sources told Reuters.

“We strongly support national preemption and believe federal fair access legislation or regulation would be a prudent move to address account closures in a consistent way across all states,” the Bank Policy Institute, an industry association, told the news organization in a statement.

JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) said it supports a national standard "that expressly prohibits political or religious discrimination in banking," Reuters said. Citigroup (NYSE:C), Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC), Bank of America (NYSE:BAC), Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS), and Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) either declined to comment or didn't respond to Reuters' request for comment.

In Friday premarket trading, JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM) stock rose 0.3%, Citi (NYSE:C) +0.3%, Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) +0.4%, Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) +0.5%, Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) +0.7%, Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) +0.5%. 

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Tesla to integrate Deepseek, Doubao AI in Chinese EVs -- Bloomberg
data.description:
• Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) plans to integrate domestically developed artificial intelligence into its Chinese electric vehicles to power voice commands and provide a more “localized” experience for drivers.
  • According to a document reviewed by Bloomberg, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) will use technology powered by Deepseek and Bytedance unit Doubao to control such things as the vehicle’s temperature, navigation, and in-car entertainment. Voice commands will be processed by Doubao while the Deepseek chat will handle more advanced AI interaction.
  • The decision to source locally rather than equip Chinese-made Tesla’s (NASDAQ:TSLA) with xAI’s Grok reflects Tesla’s (TSLA) eagerness to compete with Chinese rivals like BYD (OTCPK:BYDDY), as well as Beijing’s strict data security laws.
  • Chinese regulations would prevent the carmaker from routing data to overseas servers, essentially limiting the use of Grok in any vehicle operated in China.

data.title: ETHZilla falls 38% premarket on offering of up to 74.8M shares
data.description:
ETHZilla (NASDAQ:ETHZ) shares are down nearly 38% in premarket trading on Friday after Ether treasury company’s shareholders filed to offer up to 74.8 million convertible shares.

The company, formerly known as 180 Life Sciences, gained 164% so far this year. However, if premarket declines hold ETHZilla could lose all those gains.

Earlier in August, the company closed its private placement, raising $425M in gross proceeds through a private investment in public equity transaction for the purchase and sale of common stock and pre-funded warrants.

It announced that it intends to begin purchasing Ethereum and deploy a large percentage of the $425M in addition to continuing its prior legacy iGaming operations.

The company also announced in August that it has total holdings of 82,186 Ether at an average acquisition price of $3,806.71, which is now valued at about $349 million.

data.title: Novo Nordisk to cut staff bonuses in its home country amid growth concerns
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NVO) will be forced to lower employee bonuses in its home country, Denmark, as the company doubles down on a cost-cutting drive following a cut to its growth outlook this year, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing a company spokeswoman.

The reduction for 2025 yearly bonuses will apply to employees who are entitled to short-term incentives, the spokeswoman added.

The decision comes amid an ongoing cost-cutting initiative spearheaded by the new CEO, Maziar Mike Doustdar, who assumed leadership in August and pledged to revamp Denmark’s most valuable company.

In July, alongside Doustdar’s appointment, the weight loss drugmaker lowered its full-year growth outlook for sales and operating profit, citing, among other things, pressure on its obesity franchise led by its GLP-1 medication semaglutide, marketed as Ozempic and Wegovy.

Earlier this week, Novo Nordisk (NVO) started a global hiring freeze for non-critical business roles in an attempt to reduce expenses. The decision on bonuses was first reported by the Danish newspaper Berlingske.

data.title: Guggenheim moves to the sidelines on Paramount Skydance after recent stock rally
data.description:
Analysts at Guggenheim downgraded Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) to "neutral" from a previous investment rating of "buy" on Friday, citing a significant stock rally since initiating coverage on the company earlier this month.

PSKY does not have a price target assigned to it at the moment. It previously had a PT of $13. Shares of the company have climbed 45.2% since the merger closed.

The research firm said its core view on the company is still unchanged, and they expect the new leadership to reinvigorate the content engine, improve technology solutions, and drive incremental efficiencies.

"We believe that the Skydance team thoroughly evaluated inputs—including the pace of cord-cutting pressure and realistic views of cost savings—in setting initial profitability goals on July 8, 2024, though we do expect 2026 profitability to be below that outlook given recent investments, including a multi-year rights agreement with the UFC," the research firm said in its note.

However, they believe that key strategic questions remain unanswered, particularly related to the balance of linear and direct-to-consumer economics as an increasing number of competitor DTC apps like ESPN and Fox One are made available to pay-TV customers with bundle subscriptions.

"Driving DTC growth without pressuring linear economics is an incremental risk to consolidated performance that we believe may be underappreciated by management," the research firm said.

data.title: Ubiquiti surges as Q4 results top estimates, boosts buyback program by $500M
data.description:
• Ubiquiti (NYSE:UI) shares surged 10% in premarket trading on Friday after its fiscal fourth-quarter results topped estimates by a wide margin.
  • The networking company said that for the fiscal year ended June 30, it earned an adjusted $3.54 per share as revenue surged 49.6% year-over-year to $759.2M. Ubiquiti said the growth came from its Enterprise Technology platform, and to a lesser extent, its Service Provider Technology platform.
  • In addition, Ubiquiti said its board of directors authorized a new share buyback program for up to $500M. The company also said it would pay a quarterly dividend of at least $0.80 per share in each quarter of fiscal 2026.
